phases.label: Phase 1 Lattice
phases.subtitle: separable interference / lattice packet
phases.description: Base + A_delta, Base + B_delta, and Base + A_delta + B_delta under the frozen lattice packet.
phases.phase_note: Strongest sector-wide rung. Positive activation lift across all seven rows and full 1.0000 input-cohesion closure.

phases.label: Phase 2 Null
phases.subtitle: matched neutral / null control
phases.description: Same three-lane geometry and deviation math with ordinary administrative/checklist language instead of the lattice packet.
phases.phase_note: Discriminator rung. Hermes behaves like a strong lattice-vs-null discriminator, while Qwen and Mistral remain tracked exceptions.

phases.label: Phase 3 Non-Classical Variable
phases.subtitle: ordinary semantic/coherent continuity control
phases.description: Same three-lane V7 math with the phase-3 ordinary semantic/coherent variable packet.
phases.phase_note: This is the non-classical-variable rung, not a final claim seal. It preserves the math while asking whether ordinary semantic continuity can recreate the lattice-style lift.

nuance_lines: Phase 3 is non-classical variable, not a renamed semantic-counter claim.
Input cohesion can close at 1.0000 in lattice and null without proving the same response-side interaction.
The current discriminating feature is response-side activation lift, not closure alone.
Qwen and Mistral remain honest exceptions; they are not to be hidden or flattened away.
Semantic counter remains a later matched condition, not a retroactive relabeling of phase 3.

next_stages.label: Exception variance + random floor
next_stages.detail: Resolve the tracked exceptions before claiming uniform tightening: Qwen lattice-vs-null variance, Mistral null variance, and any needed confirmation on Trini or Nemotron edge cases. Add a matched-length random prompt floor so the lower bound of normal variance is explicit.

next_stages.label: Semantic counter condition
next_stages.detail: Build a matched-length, high-coherence, meaningful control that uses rich language without the lattice vocabulary or packet structure. If that also produces positive activation lift, semantic richness remains a competing explanation.

next_stages.label: Order / non-commutativity
next_stages.detail: Run A_delta -> B_delta versus B_delta -> A_delta with the same frozen separable harness. The question is whether the state path depends on sequence order, not just prompt mass.

next_stages.label: Contextuality
next_stages.detail: Place the same phrase inside different surrounding packet fields and measure whether activation/coherence shifts with context.

next_stages.label: V8 residual-stream probes
next_stages.detail: On open checkpoints such as Gemma and Mistral, extract residual-stream activations at matched token positions for lattice, null, and semantic-counter conditions. This is the bridge from external behavioral scoring to internal representational evidence.

next_stages.label: Rung 1 claim threshold
next_stages.detail: If lattice stays stronger than null and semantic-counter, and the order/context controls hold, the defensible next claim is reproducible interaction structure beyond naive additive prompt accumulation across model families.

next_stages.label: Rung 2 later
next_stages.detail: Formal semantic Bell/CHSH-style contextuality protocol with bounded outcomes and a classical comparison bound.

next_stages.label: Rung 3 later
next_stages.detail: Physical-observable bridge via ARC15 / HRV1.0 / quantum hardware or other synchronized sensors. This stays auxiliary until the semantic controls survive.

order.artifact_note: DeepSeek was replaced with the isolated rerun because the first BA final-stage row timed out and produced a false zero-activation artifact.
